meta.name: Soft Dinner Rolls
meta.subtitle: Tender, fluffy, buttery • everyday rolls • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: Classic soft pull-apart dinner rolls. Works as 12 standard rolls or 18 smaller rolls. Includes fresh-milled guidance, make-ahead, and freezer notes.

yield.notes: Makes 12 standard rolls (9x13 pan) or 18 smaller rolls (same dough, smaller portions).

tasteProfile.notes: Soft crumb, buttery finish, ideal for sopping gravies or making small sandwiches.

ingredients.label: Flour (bread flour preferred; or all-purpose; or fresh milled wheat flour—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: Bread flour gives taller rolls; all-purpose yields softer, slightly less chewy rolls.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (or active dry yeast)
ingredients.notes: About 2 1/4 teaspoons. If using active dry, bloom in warm milk (see steps).

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons. Helps browning and softness.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, melted (for brushing after baking)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons. Optional but highly recommended.

steps.title: Optional: bloom active dry yeast (if using)
instructions: If using active dry yeast, stir yeast and a pinch of sugar into the warm milk (use from recipe milk).
Rest 5–10 minutes until foamy. If using instant yeast, skip blooming.

steps.title: Mix the dough
instructions: In a large bowl (or mixer bowl), combine flour, sugar, salt, and (if using instant) yeast.
Add warm milk, egg, and softened butter.
Mix until a shaggy dough forms and no dry flour remains.

steps.title: Knead until smooth and elastic
instructions: Knead until dough is smooth, elastic, and slightly tacky.
Stand mixer: 6–8 minutes on low to medium-low with dough hook.
By hand: 10–12 minutes kneading.
If dough is very sticky, add flour 5–10g at a time (avoid adding too much—soft dough makes soft rolls).

steps.title: First rise (bulk)
instructions: Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and rise until doubled, 60–90 minutes depending on room temperature.

steps.title: Divide and shape
instructions: Grease a 9x13 pan (or line with parchment).
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and gently deflate.
Divide into 12 equal pieces (~70–75g each) for standard rolls, OR 18 pieces (~48–52g each) for smaller rolls.
Shape each piece into a smooth ball by pinching seams underneath and rolling lightly against the surface.
Arrange rolls evenly spaced in the pan.

steps.title: Final proof
instructions: Cover and proof until rolls are very puffy and touching or nearly touching, 45–75 minutes.
The dough should spring back slowly when gently pressed.

steps.title: Bake
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Bake 18–22 minutes (12-roll batch) until golden brown.
For smaller 18-roll batch: start checking at 15–18 minutes.
Internal temp target: 190–200°F (88–93°C).

steps.title: Brush with butter and serve
instructions: Immediately brush hot rolls with melted butter (optional but recommended).
Optional: sprinkle lightly with flaky salt while butter is still wet.
Cool 10 minutes before serving for best texture.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour can work well in rolls but may need slightly more liquid and/or a blend for maximum fluffiness.
guidelines: For the softest rolls: use 70% fresh milled flour + 30% bread flour (same total flour weight).
If using 100% fresh milled (unsifted), consider sifting out some bran and/or adding 10–25g more milk if dough feels stiff after mixing.
Fresh milled dough may benefit from a 10-minute rest after initial mixing (before kneading) to hydrate fully.
Avoid adding lots of extra flour during kneading—soft dough = soft rolls.

variations.name: Honey butter rolls
variations.change: Replace sugar with 40g honey. Reduce milk by ~10g. Brush finished rolls with honey-butter (butter + honey).

variations.name: Garlic herb rolls
variations.change: Brush baked rolls with garlic-herb butter (melted butter + garlic + parsley).

variations.name: Whole wheat blend
variations.change: Replace 150g of the flour with whole wheat flour (or fresh milled). Add +10–20g milk if needed.

storage.counter: Store airtight up to 3 days. Rewarm briefly for best softness.
storage.freezer: Freeze cooled rolls up to 2 months. Thaw at room temp; warm in 300°F (150°C) oven for 8–12 minutes.
storage.reheat: Wrap in foil and warm in 300°F (150°C) oven, or microwave briefly (texture best with oven).

notes: If rolls are browning too quickly, tent loosely with foil for the last 5–7 minutes.
If rolls feel dense, increase proof time (final proof especially) or ensure yeast is fresh and milk wasn’t too hot.
For pull-apart softness, bake rolls close together in a pan rather than spaced apart on a sheet.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions.
